concerns.text: Task T018 (US2) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depends on the output of T015 (US1) which generates 'data/processed/cleaned_ebsd.parquet'. The 'Independent Test' for US2 explicitly states it processes a 'known benchmark dataset', but the implementation task T018 implies processing the pipeline output. If T018 runs before T015 completes, it has no input data. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule. T018 cannot be [P] relative to T015.

Task T025 (US3) is marked [P] but depends on the output of T021 (US2) ('data/processed/descriptors.csv'). The 'Independent Test' for US3 requires a 'tidy dataset of (material, reduction, texture metrics)', which is the output of US2. T025 cannot run in parallel with T021 or T018. The [P] tag is incorrect here as it implies no dependency on the US2 artifact flow.

Task T033 (US4) is marked [P] but depends on the model output from T025/T027 (US3) to perform sensitivity analysis on 'interpolation tolerance' and 'R² variation'. The 'Independent Test' for US4 requires running sensitivity analysis on the model. T033 cannot be parallel-safe relative to the US3 model training tasks. The [P] tag is invalid.

Task T009 and T010 (US1 Tests) are marked [P] and placed before implementation tasks T011-T015. While the 'TDD' note suggests writing tests first, the [P] tag implies they can run in parallel with implementation. However, contract tests (T009) depend on the schema defined in T007 (Foundational), which is fine, but integration tests (T010) depend on the implementation of T011/T012. If T010 runs before T011/T012, it will fail (as intended by TDD), but the [P] tag suggests they are independent of each other. The dependency is 'Test depends on Implementation' for execution, even if written first. The [P] tag is misleading for the execution phase.

Task T037 (US4) introduces 'pole figure validation logic to compare ML predictions against physical diffraction evidence'. The spec (FR-001 to FR-009) and plan only define validation against synthetic datasets and known qualitative trends. There is no artifact or data source defined in the plan for 'physical diffraction evidence' (external to the synthetic generator). This task has no producer for its input data, making it impossible to execute as described. It violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule by assuming an external artifact that is not part of the defined data flow.
